items.title: GLM-5.2 is probably the most powerful text-only open weights LLM
items.summary: Chinese AI lab Z.ai released GLM-5.2 to their coding plan subscribers on June 13th, and then yesterday (June 16th) released the full open weights under an MIT license. Similar in size to their previous GLM-5 and GLM-5.1 releases, this is 753B parameter, 1.51TB monster - with 40 active parameters (Mixture of Experts)....

items.title: The Korean Telecom Giant at the Center of Anthropic’s Mythos Controversy
items.summary: Days before Anthropic took its most advanced AI models offline, the White House ordered the company to revoke SK Telecom’s access to Claude Mythos over claims of alleged ties to China.

items.title: Amazon SageMaker AI Async Inference now supports inline request payloads
items.summary: Today, we’re announcing inline payload support for Amazon SageMaker AI Async Inference. Customers can now send inference payloads directly in the request body of the InvokeEndpointAsync API, removing the need to upload input data to Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) before each invocation.

items.title: In game theory, generalists sometimes win out over specialists
items.summary: Researchers show that for certain kinds of games, an overlooked class of algorithms performs much better than expected.

items.title: Microsoft researcher builds a working neural network out of goats in Age of Empires II to critique AI science
items.summary: A Microsoft researcher built a working neural network out of goats, bridges, and ice ramps in the Age of Empires II map editor. What looks like a joke is a pointed critique of AI research methods. His analysis of 315 papers found that more than half already assume language models have human-like traits before the...

items.title: Amazon, Nvidia, and AMD bet $310 million on AI startup building 3D world models
items.summary: Amazon, Nvidia, and AMD are putting $310 million into world model startup Odyssey ML, now valued at $1.45 billion. CIA-linked fund IQT and Google chief scientist Jeff Dean are also backing the round. World models are shaping up to be the next big AI bet after pure language models. The article Amazon, Nvidia, and AMD...

items.title: Zhipu AI's GLM-5.2 closes in on closed-source leaders in coding marathons
items.summary: Chinese AI lab Zhipu AI releases GLM-5.2 with a stable 1-million-token context under the MIT license. On FrontierSWE, a benchmark for hours-long coding tasks, the open-source model trails Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 by just one percentage point. On reasoning, it still falls well behind closed-source rivals. The...

items.title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
items.summary: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled...

items.title: The White House Wants Anthropic to Block All Jailbreaks. That May Not Be Possible
items.summary: Trump administration officials tell WIRED that if Anthropic wants to rerelease Fable 5, it will need to ensure the model's guardrails can't be circumvented. Security experts say that can't be done.

items.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
items.summary: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.

items.title: Ten months later, the $100 Google Home Speaker is finally available for preorder
items.summary: Google's new smart speaker is more about Gemini than audio quality.

items.title: Upsampling method sharpens AI vision with up to 16 times less GPU memory
items.summary: From facial recognition on smartphones to humanoid robots, computer vision technology, which serves as the eyes of artificial intelligence (AI), is widely used in daily life. A joint research team from KAIST and international institutions has developed a technology that allows AI to see the world more clearly with...

items.title: New research shows how AMIE, our medical AI, could help manage health conditions.
items.summary: Research in “Nature” shows our conversational AI system matches primary care physicians in complex disease management.

items.title: Nvidia research shows robots that train themselves through AI coding agents
items.summary: Researchers from Nvidia, Carnegie Mellon University, and UC Berkeley are using AI coding agents to teach robots dexterous grasping in the real world. A fleet of eight robots hits up to 99 percent success on tricky tasks. The article Nvidia research shows robots that train themselves through AI coding agents appeared...

items.title: OpenAI researchers want to predict how often AI models will fail before launch
items.summary: OpenAI researchers propose a method for predicting how often a new AI model will make mistakes after release. It could fill gaps left by standard safety testing. The article OpenAI researchers want to predict how often AI models will fail before launch appeared first on The Decoder .

items.title: The Gemini-Powered Google Home Speaker Is Finally Here
items.summary: Arriving six years after Google’s last smart speaker, the new HomePod-style device was redesigned to play host to Gemini’s chatbot.

items.title: Hyperscalers may soon be unable to fund their AI buildout from cash flow alone
items.summary: According to an Epoch AI analysis, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, and Oracle are growing their AI infrastructure spending by about 70 percent a year, while operating cash flow is only rising at 23 percent. If the trend holds, spending could overtake cash flow as early as Q3 2026. Several of these companies are...

items.title: WeChat and Alipay Counterattack Against Doubao: Turning Mini-Programs Into AI Skills
